questions.question: What separates an audio-language model (LALM) from pure ASR?
options: LALMs run faster on the same hardware
LALMs produce free-form natural-language ANSWERS, not just transcripts — joint reasoning over speech, environmental sound, and music ("is the person in danger?" from a bark + yell + silence)
ASR supports more languages
LALMs only process music
questions.explanation: One model, one prompt, multiple axes: transcription, semantic reasoning, music understanding, and long-audio retrieval ("where in this 90-minute lecture...").

questions.question: What is the three-component template every 2026 LALM follows?
options: Tokenizer, retriever, generator
Audio encoder (Whisper/BEATs/CLAP/WavLM) → projector (linear or MLP into the LLM's token-embedding space) → LLM decoder consuming interleaved text + audio tokens and generating text
Three stacked Whisper models
VAD, diarizer, and transcriber
questions.explanation: Same skeleton as vision-language models, swapped frontend. Stage 1 trains only the projector on ASR/captioning; stage 2 instruction-tunes; optional stage 3 adds a speech decoder for voice-out.

questions.question: How closed is the open-vs-closed gap for audio-language models in 2026?
options: Closed models lead by 30+ points everywhere
Essentially closed: Qwen2.5-Omni-7B matches GPT-4o Audio on MMAU-Pro and Audio Flamingo Next beats Gemini 2.5 Pro on LongAudioBench — EXCEPT multi-audio tasks, where everyone is near random (~21-22%)
Open models lead everywhere by far
No benchmarks exist to compare them
questions.explanation: The multi-audio column is the universal weakness: even Gemini 2.5 Pro scores ~22% on MMAU-Pro's multi-audio subset — reasoning across multiple separate clips remains unsolved.

questions.question: What does MMAU-Pro measure?
options: Pure transcription accuracy
1800 QA pairs spanning speech, sound, music, and mixed categories — including a multi-audio subset — with per-category breakdowns (e.g., Gemini 2.5 Pro: 73.4% speech but 51.9% sound)
Audio codec compression ratios
Latency of streaming pipelines
questions.explanation: The category spread matters: models cluster much higher on speech than on environmental sound, exposing where the Whisper-style speech-dominant pretraining shows through.

questions.question: Which open LALMs should you know, and what differs across them?
options: Only one open LALM exists

Qwen2.5-Omni/Qwen3-Omni (Apache-2.0, text + speech out), SALMONN (Whisper + BEATs dual encoder), LTU and GAMA (Llama-based, text out), and Audio Flamingo 3/Next (AF-CLAP encoder, NVIDIA non-commercial) — differing in encoder, output modality, and license
They all share the same encoder
All open models are English-only
questions.explanation: Encoder choice (Whisper vs BEATs vs CLAP variants) tracks the target distribution, output modality decides whether the model can talk back, and license decides what you can ship.
